content:
Key Findings

  • Lancaster City Council's spending records cover a total of £185.7M across five financial years, from 2021/22 to 2025/26.
  • The council processed 32,574 individual transactions during this period, engaging with 2,088 unique suppliers.
  • Overall spending increased by £19.4M, representing a 75.4% rise from 2021 to 2025.
  • A significant spending increase of +£14.8M, or +59.3%, occurred between 2022 and 2023.
  • Spending is projected to decrease by £-5,062,832, or -10.1%, from 2024 to 2025.

Understanding Lancaster's Spending Data

Lancaster City Council has published extensive spending data, offering a window into its financial operations. The records span five financial years, from 2021/22 to 2025/26. This comprehensive dataset includes a total expenditure of £185.7M. Over this period, the council recorded 32,574 individual transactions. These transactions involved a wide network of suppliers, with 2,088 unique organisations receiving payments from the council.

The publication of this data aligns with the Local Government Transparency Code, which aims to make council spending more accessible to the public. By detailing every transaction, residents can scrutinise how public funds are allocated. The sheer volume of 32,574 transactions highlights the complexity and scale of the council's financial activities. Understanding these figures is crucial for effective public oversight and ensuring accountability in local government spending.

Analysing Fluctuations in Council Expenditure

An in-depth look at Lancaster City Council's spending reveals notable year-over-year fluctuations. The total expenditure for 2021 was £25.7M. This figure saw a slight decrease in 2022, falling by £-723,117, a change of -2.8%, to reach £25.0M. However, the subsequent years show substantial increases in spending, indicating shifts in council priorities or operational costs.

The period from 2022 to 2024 marked a significant expansion in the council's financial outlay. Spending surged by +£14.8M, or +59.3%, between 2022 and 2023, reaching £39.8M. This upward trend continued into the following year, with an additional increase of +£10.3M, or +26.0%, bringing the 2024 expenditure to £50.1M. This represents a substantial growth in the council's financial commitments over these two years. However, projections for 2025 indicate a reversal, with spending expected to decrease by £-5,062,832, a reduction of -10.1%, to £45.1M.

Overall, the council's spending increased by £19.4M from 2021 to 2025, representing a 75.4% rise over the five-year period. This significant change underscores the dynamic nature of local government finances. The table below provides a detailed breakdown of the year-over-year spending figures, illustrating these changes clearly.

Financial Year Total Spend Year-over-Year Change Percentage Change
2021 £25.7M N/A N/A
2022 £25.0M £-723,117 -2.8%
2023 £39.8M +£14.8M +59.3%
2024 £50.1M +£10.3M +26.0%
2025 £45.1M £-5,062,832 -10.1%

The Breadth of Published Information

The availability of 32,574 transactions and data on 2,088 unique suppliers provides a comprehensive picture of Lancaster City Council's financial interactions. This level of detail allows for granular analysis of spending patterns, identifying key areas of expenditure and the organisations that benefit from council contracts. The transparency framework encourages councils to publish this information, fostering trust and enabling public scrutiny.

While the data offers a broad overview, further investigation can reveal the specific nature of these transactions. Understanding why spending increased by +£14.8M in 2023 and by another +£10.3M in 2024 is vital for residents. Similarly, the projected decrease of £-5,062,832 for 2025 warrants attention to understand its implications for council services. This detailed financial information forms the bedrock for an informed citizenry, capable of holding their local authority to account.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Lancaster City Council about the reasons behind the significant spending increase between 2022 and 2024.
  • Attend council committee meetings, as they are open to the public, to hear discussions on financial planning and budget allocations.
  • Check the council's published contracts register to find more details about agreements with the 2,088 unique suppliers.
  • Write to your local councillor about the council's financial planning for future years, especially given the projected decrease in 2025.
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data for Lancaster City Council and other Lancashire councils yourself.
This article is based on Lancaster City Council's published spending data covering 2021/22 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
